name: RhinoList<T>
summary: Represents a list of generic data. This class is similar to System.Collections.Generic.List(T) but exposes a few more methods.

constructors.name: RhinoList<T>(IEnumerable<T>)
constructors.summary: Initializes this list as a shallow duplicate of another list, array or any other enumerable set of T.

params.name: collection
params.doc: Collection of items to duplicate.

constructors.name: RhinoList<T>(Int32)
constructors.summary: Initializes an empty list with a certain capacity.

params.name: initialCapacity
params.doc: Number of items this list can store without resizing.

constructors.name: RhinoList<T>(RhinoList<T>)
constructors.summary: Initializes an new list by shallow duplicating another list.

params.name: list
params.doc: List to mimic.

constructors.name: RhinoList<T>(Int32, T)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new list with a specified amount of values.

params.name: amount
params.doc: Number of values to add to this list. Must be equal to or larger than zero.

params.name: defaultValue
params.doc: Value to add, for reference types, the same item will be added over and over again.

methods.name: Add
methods.summary: Adds an object to the end of the List.

params.name: item
params.doc: Item to append.

methods.name: AddRange(IEnumerable<T>)
methods.summary: Adds the elements of the specified collection to the end of the List.

params.name: collection
params.doc: The collection whose elements should be added to the end of the List. The collection itself cannot be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic), but it can contain elements that are a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic), if type T is a reference type.

methods.name: AddRange(IEnumerable)
methods.summary: Adds the elements of the specified collection to the end of the List.

params.name: collection
params.doc: The collection whose elements should be added to the end of the List. The collection itself cannot be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic), but it can contain elements that are a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic). Objects in collection which cannot be represented as T will throw exceptions.

methods.name: AsReadOnly
methods.summary: Constructs a read-only wrapper of this class.

returns.doc: A wrapper.

methods.name: BinarySearch(T)
methods.summary: Searches the entire sorted List for an element using the default comparer and returns the zero-based index of the element.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to locate. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of item in the sorted List, if item is found; otherwise, a negative number that is the bitwise complement of the index of the next element that is larger than item or, if there is no larger element, the bitwise complement of Count.

methods.name: BinarySearch(T, IComparer<T>)
methods.signature: public int BinarySearch(T item, IComparer<T> comparer)
methods.summary: Searches the entire sorted List for an element using the specified comparer and returns the zero-based index of the element.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to locate. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

params.name: comparer
params.doc: The IComparer(T) implementation to use when comparing elements. Or a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) to use the default comparer Comparer(T)::Default.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of item in the sorted List, if item is found; otherwise, a negative number that is the bitwise complement of the index of the next element that is larger than item or, if there is no larger element, the bitwise complement of Count.

methods.name: BinarySearch(Int32, Int32, T, IComparer<T>)
methods.signature: public int BinarySearch(int index, int count, T item, IComparer<T> comparer)
methods.summary: Searches the entire sorted List for an element using the specified comparer and returns the zero-based index of the element.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based starting index of the range to search.

params.name: count
params.doc: The length of the range to search.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to locate. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

params.name: comparer
params.doc: The IComparer(T) implementation to use when comparing elements. Or a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) to use the default comparer Comparer(T)::Default.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of item in the sorted List, if item is found; otherwise, a negative number that is the bitwise complement of the index of the next element that is larger than item or, if there is no larger element, the bitwise complement of Count.

methods.name: Clear
methods.summary: Removes all elements from the List.

methods.name: Contains
methods.summary: Determines whether an element is in the List.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to locate in the List. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

returns.doc: true if item is found in the List; otherwise, false.

methods.name: ConvertAll<TOutput>
methods.summary: Aggregates all results of a conversion function over this table into a new list.

params.name: converter
params.doc: A conversion function that can transform from T to TOutput.

returns.doc: The new list.

methods.name: CopyTo(T[])
methods.summary: Copies the entire List to a compatible one-dimensional array, starting at the beginning of the target array.

params.name: array
params.doc: The one-dimensional Array that is the destination of the elements copied from List. The Array must have zero-based indexing.

methods.name: CopyTo(T[], Int32)
methods.signature: public void CopyTo(T[] array, int arrayIndex)
methods.summary: Copies the entire List to a compatible one-dimensional array, starting at the specified index of the target array.

params.name: array
params.doc: The one-dimensional Array that is the destination of the elements copied from List. The Array must have zero-based indexing.

params.name: arrayIndex
params.doc: The zero-based index in array at which copying begins.

methods.name: CopyTo(Int32, T[], Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public void CopyTo(int index, T[] array, int arrayIndex, int count)
methods.summary: Copies a range of elements from the List to a compatible one-dimensional array, starting at the specified index of the target array.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based index in the source List at which copying begins.

params.name: array
params.doc: The one-dimensional Array that is the destination of the elements copied from List. The Array must have zero-based indexing.

params.name: arrayIndex
params.doc: The zero-based index in array at which copying begins.

params.name: count
params.doc: The number of elements to copy.

methods.name: Duplicate
methods.summary: Returns a shallow copy of this instance. If the generic type is comprised of only value types (struct, enum, ptr), then the result will be a deep copy.

returns.doc: The duplicated list.

methods.name: Exists
methods.summary: Determines whether the List contains elements that match the conditions defined by the specified predicate.

params.name: match
params.doc: The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the elements to search for.

returns.doc: true if the List contains one or more elements that match the conditions defined by the specified predicate; otherwise, false.

methods.name: Find
methods.summary: Searches for an element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, and returns the first occurrence within the entire List.

params.name: match
params.doc: The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the element to search for.

returns.doc: The first element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, if found; otherwise, the default value for type T.

methods.name: FindAll
methods.summary: Retrieves all the elements that match the conditions defined by the specified predicate.

params.name: match
params.doc: The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the elements to search for.

returns.doc: A ON_List(T) containing all the elements that match the conditions defined by the specified predicate, if found; otherwise, an empty ON_List(T).

methods.name: FindIndex(Predicate<T>)
methods.summary: Searches for an element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, and returns the zero-based index of the first occurrence within the entire List.

params.name: match
params.doc: The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the element to search for.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of the first occurrence of an element that matches the conditions defined by match, if found; otherwise, -1.

methods.name: FindIndex(Int32, Predicate<T>)
methods.signature: public int FindIndex(int startIndex, Predicate<T> match)
methods.summary: Searches for an element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, and returns the zero-based index of the first occurrence within the entire List.

params.name: startIndex
params.doc: The zero-based starting index of the search.

params.name: match
params.doc: The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the element to search for.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of the first occurrence of an element that matches the conditions defined by match, if found; otherwise, -1.

methods.name: FindIndex(Int32, Int32, Predicate<T>)
methods.signature: public int FindIndex(int startIndex, int count, Predicate<T> match)
methods.summary: Searches for an element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, and returns the zero-based index of the first occurrence within the range of elements in the List that extends from the specified index to the last element.

params.name: startIndex
params.doc: The zero-based starting index of the search.

params.name: count
params.doc: The number of elements in the section to search.

params.name: match
params.doc: The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the element to search for.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of the first occurrence of an element that matches the conditions defined by match, if found; otherwise, -1.

methods.name: FindLast
methods.summary: Searches for an element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, and returns the last occurrence within the entire List.

params.name: match
params.doc: The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the element to search for.

returns.doc: The last element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, if found; otherwise, the default value for type T.

methods.name: FindLastIndex(Predicate<T>)
methods.summary: Searches for an element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, and returns the zero-based index of the last occurrence within the entire List.

params.name: match
params.doc: The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the element to search for.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of the last occurrence of an element that matches the conditions defined by match, if found; otherwise, -1.

methods.name: FindLastIndex(Int32, Predicate<T>)
methods.signature: public int FindLastIndex(int startIndex, Predicate<T> match)
methods.summary: Searches for an element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, and returns the zero-based index of the last occurrence within the entire List.

params.name: startIndex
params.doc: The zero-based starting index of the backward search.

params.name: match
params.doc: The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the element to search for.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of the last occurrence of an element that matches the conditions defined by match, if found; otherwise, -1.

methods.name: FindLastIndex(Int32, Int32, Predicate<T>)
methods.signature: public int FindLastIndex(int startIndex, int count, Predicate<T> match)
methods.summary: Searches for an element that matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate, and returns the zero-based index of the last occurrence within the entire List.

params.name: startIndex
params.doc: The zero-based starting index of the backward search.

params.name: count
params.doc: The number of elements in the section to search.

params.name: match
params.doc: The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the element to search for.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of the last occurrence of an element that matches the conditions defined by match, if found; otherwise, -1.

methods.name: ForEach
methods.summary: Performs the specified action on each element of the List.

params.name: action
params.doc: The Action(T) delegate to perform on each element of the List.

methods.name: GetEnumerator
methods.summary: Constructs an enumerator that is capable of iterating over all items in this list.

returns.doc: The new enumerator.

methods.name: GetRange
methods.signature: public RhinoList<T> GetRange(int index, int count)
methods.summary: Constructs a shallow copy of a range of elements in the source List.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based List index at which the range starts.

params.name: count
params.doc: The number of elements in the range.

returns.doc: A shallow copy of a range of elements in the source List.

methods.name: IndexOf(T)
methods.summary: Searches for the specified object and returns the zero-based index of the first occurrence within the entire List.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to locate in the List. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of the first occurrence of item within the entire List, if found; otherwise, -1.

methods.name: IndexOf(T, Int32)
methods.signature: public int IndexOf(T item, int index)
methods.summary: Searches for the specified object and returns the zero-based index of the first occurrence within the range of elements in the List that extends from the specified index to the last element.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to locate in the List. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based starting index of the search.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of the first occurrence of item within the entire List, if found; otherwise, -1.

methods.name: IndexOf(T, Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public int IndexOf(T item, int index, int count)
methods.summary: Searches for the specified object and returns the zero-based index of the first occurrence within the range of elements in the List that starts at the specified index and contains the specified number of elements.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to locate in the List. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based starting index of the search.

params.name: count
params.doc: The number of elements in the section to search.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of the first occurrence of item within the entire List, if found; otherwise, -1.

methods.name: Insert
methods.signature: public void Insert(int index, T item)
methods.summary: Inserts an element into the List at the specified index.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based index at which item should be inserted.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to insert. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

methods.name: InsertRange
methods.signature: public void InsertRange(int index, IEnumerable<T> collection)
methods.summary: Inserts the elements of a collection into the List at the specified index.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based index at which the new elements should be inserted.

params.name: collection
params.doc: The collection whose elements should be inserted into the List. The collection itself cannot be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic), but it can contain elements that are a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic), if type T is a reference type.

methods.name: LastIndexOf(T)
methods.summary: Searches for the specified object and returns the zero-based index of the last occurrence within the entire List.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to locate in the List. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of the last occurrence of item within the entire the List, if found; otherwise, -1.

methods.name: LastIndexOf(T, Int32)
methods.signature: public int LastIndexOf(T item, int index)
methods.summary: Searches for the specified object and returns the zero-based index of the last occurrence within the range of elements in the List that extends from the first element to the specified index.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to locate in the List. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based starting index of the backward search.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of the last occurrence of item within the entire the List, if found; otherwise, -1.

methods.name: LastIndexOf(T, Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public int LastIndexOf(T item, int index, int count)
methods.summary: Searches for the specified object and returns the zero-based index of the last occurrence within the range of elements in the List that contains the specified number of elements and ends at the specified index.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to locate in the List. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based starting index of the backward search.

params.name: count
params.doc: The number of elements in the section to search.

returns.doc: The zero-based index of the last occurrence of item within the entire the List, if found; otherwise, -1.

methods.name: RemapIndex
methods.summary: Remap an index in the infinite range onto the List index range.

params.name: index
params.doc: Index to remap.

returns.doc: Remapped index.

methods.name: Remove
methods.summary: Removes the first occurrence of a specific object from the List.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to remove from the List. The value can be a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) for reference types.

returns.doc: true if item is successfully removed; otherwise, false. This method also returns false if item was not found in the List.

methods.name: RemoveAll
methods.summary: Removes the all the elements that match the conditions defined by the specified predicate.

params.name: match
params.doc: The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions of the elements to remove.

returns.doc: The number of elements removed from the List.

methods.name: RemoveAt
methods.summary: Removes the element at the specified index of the List.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based index of the element to remove.

methods.name: RemoveNulls
methods.summary: Removes all elements from the List that are null references (Nothing in Visual Basic). This function will not do anything if T is not a Reference type.

returns.doc: The number of nulls removed from the List.

methods.name: RemoveRange
methods.signature: public void RemoveRange(int index, int count)
methods.summary: Removes a range of elements from the List.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based starting index of the range of elements to remove.

params.name: count
params.doc: The number of elements to remove.

methods.name: Reverse()
methods.summary: Reverses the order of the elements in the entire List.

methods.name: Reverse(Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public void Reverse(int index, int count)
methods.summary: Reverses the order of the elements in the specified range.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based starting index of the range to reverse.

params.name: count
params.doc: The number of elements in the range to reverse.

methods.name: Sort()
methods.summary: Sorts the elements in the entire List using the default comparer.

methods.name: Sort(IComparer<T>)
methods.summary: Sorts the elements in the entire list using the specified System.Comparison(T)

params.name: comparer
params.doc: The IComparer(T) implementation to use when comparing elements, or a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) to use the default comparer Comparer(T).Default.

methods.name: Sort(Comparison<T>)
methods.summary: Sorts the elements in the entire list using the specified comparer.

params.name: comparison
params.doc: The System.Comparison(T) to use when comparing elements.

methods.name: Sort(Double[])
methods.summary: Sort this list based on a list of numeric keys of equal length. The keys array will not be altered.
methods.remarks: This function does not exist on the DotNET List class. David thought it was a good idea.

params.name: keys
params.doc: Numeric keys to sort with.

methods.name: Sort(Int32[])
methods.summary: Sort this list based on a list of numeric keys of equal length. The keys array will not be altered.
methods.remarks: This function does not exist on the DotNET List class. David thought it was a good idea.

params.name: keys
params.doc: Numeric keys to sort with.

methods.name: Sort(Int32, Int32, IComparer<T>)
methods.signature: public void Sort(int index, int count, IComparer<T> comparer)
methods.summary: Sorts the elements in a range of elements in list using the specified comparer.

params.name: index
params.doc: The zero-based starting index of the range to sort.

params.name: count
params.doc: The length of the range to sort.

params.name: comparer
params.doc: The IComparer(T) implementation to use when comparing elements, or a null reference (Nothing in Visual Basic) to use the default comparer Comparer(T).Default.

methods.name: ToArray
methods.summary: Constructs an array that contains all items in this list.

returns.doc: An array containing all items in this list.

methods.name: TrimExcess
methods.summary: Sets the capacity to the actual number of elements in the List, if that number is less than a threshold value.
methods.remarks: This function differs from the DotNET implementation of List<T> since that one only trims the excess if the excess exceeds 10% of the list length.

methods.name: TrueForAll
methods.summary: Determines whether every element in the List matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate.

params.name: match
params.doc: The Predicate(T) delegate that defines the conditions to check against the elements.

returns.doc: true if every element in the List matches the conditions defined by the specified predicate; otherwise, false. If the list has no elements, the return value is true.

properties.name: Capacity
properties.summary: Gets or sets the total number of elements the internal data structure can hold without resizing.

properties.name: Count
properties.summary: Gets the number of elements actually contained in the List.

properties.name: First
properties.summary: Gets or sets the first item in the list. This is synonymous to calling List[0].

properties.name: Item
properties.summary: Gets or sets the element at the specified index.

properties.name: Last
properties.summary: Gets or sets the last item in the list. This is synonymous to calling List[Count-1].

properties.name: NullCount
properties.summary: Gets the number of null references (Nothing in Visual Basic) in this list. If T is a ValueType, this property always return zero.
